truth.papers.A45.title: Phase transformation and austenite stability during thermomechanical processing of high (~5%) Al added low-density medium Mn steel
truth.papers.A45.paper_scope: Development of a high Al-added low-density medium Mn steel and investigation of phase transformation, austenite stability under different intercritical annealing times, and correlation with tensile properties.

prediction.papers.A45.title: Phase transformation and austenite stability during thermomechanical processing of high (~5%) Al added low-density medium Mn steel
prediction.papers.A45.paper_scope: Development and characterization of high Al (~5 wt.%) added low-density medium Mn steel, focusing on phase transformation, austenite stability, microstructural evolution during thermomechanical processing and intercritical annealing, and their effects on mechanical properties and TRIP effect.

truth.alloys.alloy_designed_fe8mn5al02c.alloys: Designed composition based on SFE calculation; actual developed steel has slightly different composition.
truth.alloys.alloy_developed_fe779mn488al084si: high Al added low-density medium Mn steel
Actual composition measured by XRF and OES (Table 1). Density measured as 6.91455 g/cc (~11.35% reduction compared to conventional steel).

prediction.alloys.alloy_developed_fe779mn488al084si: Al added low-density medium Mn steel
high Al added low-density medium Mn steel

Designed targeting stacking fault energy (SFE) in the range of 20-40 mJ/m². Theoretical SFE calculated as ~35 mJ/m² using the Olson-Cohen model. Measured density is 6.91455 g/cc, corresponding to ~11.35% reduction compared to conventional steel (~7.8 g/cc).

truth.processes.proc_cast.description: Casting of the developed steel in an open-air induction furnace.
truth.processes.proc_hot_forged.description: Homogenized at 1100°C for 2 hr, hot forged to 50% thickness reduction in temperature range 1050-800°C, air cooled to room temperature.
truth.processes.proc_hrq.description: Hot rolling (soaking at 1100°C for 2 hr) in temperature range 1050-800°C, two passes with 50% reduction each (reheating at 1100°C for 5 min between passes), water quenching to room temperature from finish rolling temperature.
truth.processes.proc_hrq.processes_notes: Surface machined up to 1 mm depth on both sides to remove oxide layer.
truth.processes.proc_ia750_5min.description: Intercritically annealed at 750°C for 5 min (starting from hot rolled & quenched condition), air cooled to room temperature.
truth.processes.proc_ia750_30min.description: Intercritically annealed at 750°C for 30 min (starting from hot rolled & quenched condition), air cooled to room temperature.
truth.processes.proc_ia750_60min.description: Intercritically annealed at 750°C for 60 min (starting from hot rolled & quenched condition), air cooled to room temperature.
truth.processes.proc_ia750_120min.description: Intercritically annealed at 750°C for 120 min (starting from hot rolled & quenched condition), air cooled to room temperature.
truth.processes.proc_ia750_180min.description: Intercritically annealed at 750°C for 180 min (starting from hot rolled & quenched condition), air cooled to room temperature.

prediction.processes.proc_cast.description: As-cast condition after conventional melting-casting in an open-air induction furnace.
prediction.processes.proc_hot_forged.description: Homogenized at 1100°C for 2 hr and hot forged with 50% thickness reduction in the temperature range of 1050-800°C, followed by air cooling to room temperature.
prediction.processes.proc_hrq.description: Soaked at 1100°C for 2 hr, hot rolled in two passes with 50% thickness reduction each in the temperature range of 1050-800°C (reheated at 1100°C for 5 min between passes), water quenched from finish rolling temperature to room temperature, and surface machined by 1 mm on both sides.
prediction.processes.proc_hrq.processes_notes: Finish rolling temperature ~800°C. Final sheet thickness after machining was 3 mm.
prediction.processes.proc_ia750_5min.description: Intercritically annealed at 750°C for 5 minutes after hot rolling and quenching, followed by air cooling to room temperature.
prediction.processes.proc_ia750_30min.description: Intercritically annealed at 750°C for 30 minutes after hot rolling and quenching, followed by air cooling to room temperature.

prediction.processes.proc_ia750_180min.description: Intercritically annealed at 750°C for 180 minutes after hot rolling and quenching, followed by air cooling to room temperature.

truth.structures.sample_cast.overall_structure: Dual-phase microstructure of delta ferrite and austenite; dendritic austenite in ferrite matrix.
truth.structures.sample_forged.overall_structu: Dual-phase microstructure of delta ferrite and austenite; some dendritic structure remains.
truth.structures.sample_hrq.overall_structure: Martensite (lenticular), untransformed austenite, and delta ferrite. Delta ferrite stable, austenite partially transformed to martensite.
truth.structures.sample_ia750_5min.overall_str: Formation of intercritical ferrite and needle-shaped reverted austenite from martensite; delta ferrite remains stable.
truth.structures.sample_ia750_5min.microstruct: reduced compared to HRQ due to annealing
truth.structures.sample_ia750_30min.overall_st: Reverted austenite partially dissolves into intercritical ferrite; reverted austenite fraction decreases.
truth.structures.sample_ia750_60min.overall_st: Globular and needle-shaped reverted austenite with optimum mechanical stability; Mn-lean reverted austenite, intercritical ferrite, and untransformed austenite.
truth.structures.sample_ia750_60min.microstruc: needle: length 1.42 ± 0.16 µm, width 0.345 ± 0.40 µm; globular: average diameter 0.0185 ± 0.010 µm
truth.structures.sample_ia750_120min.overall_s: Most reverted austenite dissolved into intercritical ferrite; low reverted austenite fraction.
truth.structures.sample_ia750_180min.overall_s: Complete dissolution of reverted austenite; mainly intercritical ferrite and untransformed austenite with stable delta ferrite.

prediction.structures.sample_cast.overall_structure: Dual-phase microstructure of delta ferrite and austenite
prediction.structures.sample_forged.overall_structu: Dual-phase microstructure of delta ferrite and austenite
prediction.structures.sample_forged.microstructure_: mostly elongated with some dendritic remnants
prediction.structures.sample_hrq.overall_structure: Tri-phase microstructure of delta ferrite, austenite, and martensite
prediction.structures.sample_ia750_5min.overall_str: Delta ferrite, untransformed austenite, needle-shaped reverted austenite, and intercritical ferrite
prediction.structures.sample_ia750_5min.microstruct: needle shape; major axis 2.829±0.30 µm, minor axis 0.350±0.20 µm
prediction.structures.sample_ia750_30min.overall_st: Delta ferrite, untransformed austenite, reduced needle-shaped reverted austenite, and increased intercritical ferrite
prediction.structures.sample_ia750_30min.microstruc: needle shape; fraction decreased compared to IA750_5 Min due to dissolution into intercritical ferrite
prediction.structures.sample_ia750_60min.overall_st: Delta ferrite, untransformed austenite, needle and globular reverted austenite, and intercritical ferrite
prediction.structures.sample_ia750_60min.microstruc: Mn-rich adjacent to reverted austenite (Mn 9.83±0.37 wt.%, Al 4.04±0.13 wt.%)
needle and globular shapes; needle major axis 1.42±0.16 µm, minor axis 0.345±0.40 µm; globular average diameter 0.0185±0.010 µm
formed by dissolution of reverted austenite
prediction.structures.sample_ia750_120min.overall_s: Delta ferrite, untransformed austenite, traces of needle and globular reverted austenite, and intercritical ferrite
prediction.structures.sample_ia750_180min.overall_s: Delta ferrite, untransformed austenite, and dominant intercritical ferrite with negligible reverted austenite
prediction.structures.sample_ia750_180min.microstru: Mn and C enriched (Mn 9.42±0.10 wt.% in untransformed austenite)
dominant; prior martensite almost completely dissolved

truth.interfaces.sample_ia750_60min.phase_evol: Intercritical annealing leads to martensite dissolution; reverted austenite forms and then transforms to intercritical ferrite. Elemental partitioning: Mn and C enrich in untransformed austenite, Mn-lean reverts; δ-ferrite stable throughout.

prediction.interfaces.sample_ia750_60min.interface_: At interface: ~65% Fe, ~18% Mn, ~11% Al, ~1.7% Si, 2.40% C. Mn and C enrichment and Fe/Al depletion observed at grain boundary due to partitioning during dissolution of reverted austenite.
prediction.interfaces.sample_ia750_60min.phase_evol: During annealing, martensite dissolves into intercritical ferrite and reverted austenite; further annealing causes Mn and C to partition from reverted austenite to untransformed austenite, reducing reverted austenite stability.

truth.characterization_methods.sample_cast.key: Dual-phase, dendritic austenite in ferrite matrix.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_forged.k: Slight deviation at ~600°C during heating/cooling, indicating possible carbide dissolution; no significant contraction observed.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_ia750_60: Results in Table 3; best combination at 60 min (UTS 658.45 MPa, TE 12%).
truth.characterization_methods.sample_ia750_18: Almost complete dissolution of reverted austenite.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_cast.key: Dual-phase microstructure with dendritic austenite indicated by blue arrow in cast specimen.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_forged.k: Heating rate 10°C/s, holding at 1000°C for 5/15 min, cooling rate 1°C/s. No significant deviation except slight deviation at ~600°C during heating and cooling, attributed to carbide/cementite dissolution.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_hrq.purp: Phase identification before and after tensile test
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_hrq.key_: Rietveld analysis shows ~19% austenite before tensile test reducing to ~18% after deformation.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_ia750_5m: Phase identification before and after tensile test
Rietveld analysis shows ~46% austenite before tensile test reducing to ~42% after deformation.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_ia750_60: Phase identification before and after tensile test
Rietveld analysis shows ~35% austenite before tensile test with highest transformation ratio ~20% during deformation.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_ia750_30: Yield strength 450±10 MPa, ultimate tensile strength 615±10 MPa, total elongation 7±1 % along rolling direction.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_ia750_12: Yield strength 433±4.5 MPa, ultimate tensile strength 571±7 MPa, total elongation 5±0.4 % along rolling direction.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_ia750_18: Yield strength 427±6.5 MPa, ultimate tensile strength 570±6 MPa, total elongation 5.5±0.5 % along rolling direction.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Dilatometry curve comparison; intercritical annealing temperature chosen at 750°C based on dual-phase region.
truth.computational_details.idx_1.validation_a: SFE for designed composition ~35 mJ/m2; adjusted SFE for reverted austenite in IA750_60Min ~27 mJ/m2 based on measured composition.
truth.computational_details.idx_1.analytical_m: ΔG_chem^{γ→ε}=245.267 J/mole, ΔG_mag^{γ→ε}=0.0029319 J/mole, ΔG_ex^{γ→ε}=19.6842 J/mole, ρ=2.9328×10⁻⁵ mol/m², σ^{γ/ε}=10 mJ/m²; resulting SFE≈35 mJ/m² for equilibrium composition; SFE changes to ~27 mJ/m² for revereted austenite composition in IA750_60Min.
Equations (1)-(4) used; lattice parameter a₀=3.5945+0.00125(Mn-20)+0.00594Al+0.0272C; grain size assumed 40 µm.
truth.computational_details.idx_2.analytical_m: Measured density of developed steel: 6.91455 g/cc; ~11.35% density reduction compared to conventional steel (7.8 g/cc).

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Dilatometry test conducted on hot-forged sample to correlate theoretical critical temperatures with actual critical temperatures; slight deviation at ~600°C corresponds to carbide dissolution.
prediction.computational_details.idx_1.potential_mo: Olson and Cohen thermodynamic model including grain size, temperature, and alloy composition
prediction.computational_details.idx_1.validation_a: Theoretical SFE of designed steel calculated as ~35 mJ/m². For IA750_60 Min reverted austenite composition (Mn 7.58±0.14 wt.%, Al 3.96±0.15 wt.%), recalculated SFE is ~27 mJ/m²; if C decreases to ~0.05 wt.%, SFE drops to ~2 mJ/m². Experimentally, TRIP is observed as the dominant deformation mechanism.
prediction.computational_details.idx_1.analytical_m: Calculate stacking fault energy of austenite
Delta G_chem^gamma->epsilon: 245.267 J/mole; Delta G_mag: 0.0029319 J/mole; Delta G_ex: 19.6842 J/mole; rho: 2.9328e-5 mol/m²; interfacial energy sigma: 10 mJ/m²; grain size assumed 40 µm
SFE solely cannot determine deformation behavior; thermomechanical processing and chemical partitioning significantly alter actual SFE and deformation mechanism.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The lath martensite start temperature calculated by empirical equation is ~350°C, supporting martensite formation upon water quenching.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: In dilatometry, only a slight deviation at ~600°C was observed, attributed to possible dissolution of carbide/cementite or ferrite; no significant transformation of ferrite to austenite.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Fractography: HRQ specimen shows cleavage/flat facets (brittle failure); annealed specimens show quasi-cleavage with some dimples from reverted austenite; overall brittle due to high δ-ferrite content.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: No TWIP effect observed despite high Al; serrations absent in stress-strain curves; deformation mechanism mainly TRIP.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: SFE calculated for equilibrium austenite composition at 750°C: Al 4.39%, C 0.47%, Mn 11.16%, Si 1.09% leading to ~35 mJ/m2; but actual revereted austenite composition changes SFE to ~27 mJ/m2.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Austenite mechanical stability K calculated by Sugimoto's model: HRQ K~3.26, IA750_5Min K~1.93, IA750_30Min K~1.10, IA750_60Min K~1.92, IA750_120Min and 180Min K values higher (lower stability).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: The product of strength and elongation (PSE) is highest for IA750_60Min at 7.9 GPa%.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Fig. 8 provides a schematic representation of martensite dissolution and austenite reversion during annealing.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Fig. 9 HRTEM and EDS mapping show Mn enrichment in reverted austenite; Fig. 10 APT gives quantitative atomic concentrations across the interface.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Product of ultimate tensile strength and total elongation (PSE): HRQ 1.01 GPa%, IA750_5 Min 2.5 GPa%, IA750_30 Min 4.3 GPa%, IA750_60 Min 7.9 GPa%, IA750_120 Min 2.8 GPa%, IA750_180 Min 3.1 GPa%.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Volume fraction of austenite and mechanical stability parameter (K) before/after tensile test: HRQ ~19% to ~18% (K~3.26, transformation ratio ~5.5%); IA750_5 Min ~46% to ~42% (K~1.93, ratio 8.7%); IA750_30 Min ~40% (K 1.10); IA750_60 Min ~35% (K~1.92, ratio ~20%); IA750_120 Min and IA750_180 Min ~30%.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Work hardening rate (WHR) behavior of annealed specimens shows two stages (S1, S2). Mean WHR in stage S2: IA750_5 Min 1100-400 MPa, IA750_30 Min 1700-1000 MPa, IA750_60 Min 2000-1000 MPa, IA750_180 Min 1500-900 MPa.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Fractography of HRQ specimen exhibits mainly cleavage and flat facets indicating brittle failure. Annealed specimens (IA750_5 Min to IA750_180 Min) show quasi-cleavage fracture; delta ferrite contributes to cleavage while reverted austenite produces dimples. Majority of fracture surface remains brittle due to high delta ferrite fraction.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: No TWIP effect observed in the developed steel despite high theoretical SFE (~35 mJ/m²). Dominant deformation mechanism is TRIP. Actual SFE of IA750_60 Min reverted austenite recalculated as ~27 mJ/m², and potentially ~2 mJ/m² at lower C content (~0.05 wt.%).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Dilatometry curve of hot-forged sample shows insignificant contraction during heating/cooling, confirming stable delta ferrite remains during solidification and minimal austenite decomposition to martensite after hot forging. Slight deviation at ~600°C corresponds to carbide/cementite dissolution.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: APT quantitative concentration profile for IA750_60 Min: reverted austenite contains Fe 72.92±0.33 at.%, Mn 12.41±0.24 at.%, Al 11.74±0.32 at.%, Si 1.34±0.08 at.%, C 1.59±0.09 at.%; intercritical ferrite contains Fe 77.94±0.35 at.%, Mn 5.65±0.36 at.%, Al 14.41±0.14 at.%, Si 1.76±0.12 at.%, C 0.24±0.12 at.%. Interface composition: ~65% Fe, ~18% Mn, ~11% Al, ~1.7% Si, 2.40% C.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: The hot rolled sheet thickness before surface machining was approximately 5 mm (inferred from removal of 1 mm per side yielding final 3 mm sheet).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Figure 6 provides engineering stress-strain curves (A1), true stress-strain curves (A2), and work hardening rate curves (B1-B4) for annealed specimens, but quantitative curve coordinates are not fully tabulated in the text.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: The developed steel has a measured density of 6.91455 g/cc, achieving ~11.35% density reduction compared to conventional steel (~7.8 g/cc) by addition of ~5 wt.% Al and ~1 wt.% Si.
